name: mariadb
note: GPL-2.0. Stores proxy_accounts, traffic_logs, fake_websites, server_configs, and the Filament users table. Verify probe is the Cycle 2 / 3 drift-detection upgrade (v0.0.41) — runs `SELECT VERSION()` from inside the panel container against the live db service, and the matcher's soft version match asserts the pinned `version` field above is contained in the bare X.Y.Z-MariaDB string the daemon returns. `mariadb -BN` strips the ASCII-table formatting (-B batch, -N no-headers) so first_line(stdout) is exactly `<version>-MariaDB[-build_flavor]`. Auth via the existing app user (DB_USERNAME / DB_PASSWORD) — SELECT VERSION() is a system function that any authenticated user can call regardless of grants, so reusing the panel's existing credential avoids a dedicated `health` user provisioning step. MYSQL_PWD env-var keeps the password out of argv (would otherwise surface in `ps -ef` for the probe's lifetime — same v0.0.17 supply-chain hygiene as scripts/backup.sh:33's mariadb-dump invocation). Probe evolution: v0.0.34 introduced a TCP-open + echo (broken at the soft-version-match stage — `connected` doesn't contain `11`); v0.0.35 silenced it; v0.0.37 declared the silencing honestly via `expect_no_version_line: true`; v0.0.41 finally restores real drift detection by giving the probe an authenticated identity query. expect_zero_exit: true means Access-denied / can't-connect / cli-missing all flip the row to VerifyFailed; a deployed MariaDB whose VERSION() doesn't contain the pinned string flips to VersionMismatch.
